finding: .hedl-tier records a hardcoded absolute developer-specific path, not portable across clones.
detail: Contents: `{"tier": "team", "skill": "/home/nfoster/github/scratch/hedl/skill/hedl"}`. SKILL.md forbids hand-editing this file but the repo ships it pre-broken for any clone path.
recommendation: Either omit .hedl-tier from the dev repo, write a relative path, or have install.py / doctor regenerate when the path does not resolve.

finding: Two byte-identical config trees committed: .work/config/ and skill/hedl/work-state/config/, with no documented relationship.
detail: tiers.json projects skill/hedl/work-state/ to .work/ with on_exists:skip, so on first install they diverge — but both are committed verbatim now. Editing one silently drifts from the other.
recommendation: Symlink, document one as template-only, or remove the duplicate.

finding: CLAUDE.md says Phase 0 is active but .work/phases/phase-0.json is the literal 'EDIT ME' template; .work/work.json has active_item=null and active=[].
detail: getting-started.md tells the operator to edit these on first install. The hedl repo did not follow its own instructions and ships placeholders.
recommendation: Either fill in real Phase 0 content (the repo is mid-flight per ADRs 001-023) or move the project's own .work/ outside the framework template.

finding: Same scripts appear at two paths (.github/scripts/ as symlinks into skill/hedl/scripts/) with no upfront explanation of which to invoke.
detail: README, SKILL.md and getting-started.md mix both paths. Only tiers.md mentions the symlink relationship.
recommendation: State the rule once: install.py runs from skill/hedl/scripts/; everything else from .github/scripts/.

finding: skill/hedl/work-state/ is a template for .work/ but the name does not telegraph that.
recommendation: Rename to work-state-template/ or add a README in the directory marking it template-only.

finding: release.py, reflect.py, contribute.py are projected by lightweight tier but README and getting-started.md don't describe their purpose or when to use them.
recommendation: Add a Scripts table in README.md with purpose and the natural-language phrase that invokes each.

finding: The first gate command shown in README fails by design when run from main, but no doc warns about this; the failure message does not point back to docs.
recommendation: Note 'first run from main fails by design — make a branch first' in onboarding; or have am_i_done.py print that hint when it detects main.

finding: hedl.toml [verify] mechanism is presented but the recognised keys, defaults, and unknown-key behaviour are not documented.
recommendation: Document the recognised [verify] keys, their defaults and behaviour for unknown keys; ship an example hedl.toml.

finding: budget_manager.py thresholds use undefined units ('invocations') with no explanation of what counts as one or who is supposed to call record.
recommendation: Add a short docs/budget.md describing the unit of accounting, who records, and tuning guidance.

finding: Argument format (comma vs repeated flag vs JSON) for --agents and --optional in budget_manager.py is not documented in SKILL.md.
recommendation: Have gen_skill_metadata.py emit the value format from CLI_SPEC or include it directly.

finding: skill/hedl/workflows/ contains GitHub Actions YAML + PR template + dependabot config; the name implies Hedl flow definitions.
.gitignore opens with an Obsidian section and the repo ships a .obsidian/ directory not mentioned in docs.
recommendation: Either document the Obsidian workflow or remove the directory from the framework repo.

finding: ADR home is not unambiguously documented; docs/ and .work/decisions/ both surface ADR-shaped content.
recommendation: Add a single line stating .work/decisions/ is canonical.

finding: install.py vs am_i_done.py vs budget_manager.py — no single onboarding sentence juxtaposes the three.
recommendation: Add a three-row 'scripts you will run' table to README.
